frozen: ground truth annotated manually by the author after reading the chat, BEFORE writing any extractor; never modified afterwards

claims.what: the audience for the tool is currently vague: probably people running long-horizon projects with high context-switching cost
a knowledge-graph backend was overengineered for our needs (observed earlier)

constraints.what: no required server; tool must be local-only, offline-capable
no required online LLM call at query time to retrieve state

decisions.what: prototype against ourselves first; defer audience definition
do not lock down the audience before prototyping
keep the state structure flat for now; do not pursue a graph backend
calendar/inbox/code integrations are out of scope for v0

conflicts.what: build-time LLM extraction (richer, but vendor lock-in) vs. no LLM at all — undecided

open_questions.what: why is this not just a glorified structured-notes app — what is the genuine novelty
is automatic forgetting / surfacing-about-to-be-forgotten the killer angle
who actually uses this — concrete audience
whether to allow a cloud LLM call at build-time even if not at query-time
